title: Glass Onion: A Knives Out Mystery
reviewText: Glass Onion flexes the durability of Johnson's twist on a classic genre formula and the adaptability of its central sleuth.

title: Glass Onion: A Knives Out Mystery

title: Glass Onion: A Knives Out Mystery
reviewText: Rian Johnson's knife is sharper and more precise the second time out.

title: Glass Onion: A Knives Out Mystery
reviewText: Rian Johnson's Knives Out (2019) was a delightful surprise, and now Netflix has thrown tons of money at a semi-sequel, and too much money has ruined the concept.

title: Glass Onion: A Knives Out Mystery
reviewText: Janelle Monae, the other lead, is seventy times more magnetic than Ana De Armas was in the last film...

title: Glass Onion: A Knives Out Mystery
reviewText: The director managed to perfectly thread the needle, ensuring that 'Glass Onion' felt unique but still delighted the fans of its predecessor.

title: Glass Onion: A Knives Out Mystery
reviewText: The director managed to perfectly thread the needle, ensuring that 'Glass Onion' felt unique but still delighted the fans of its predecessor.

title: Glass Onion: A Knives Out Mystery
reviewText: Johnson's second crack at his franchise is a worthy successor to the Knives Out name that dangles suspense overhead - but not without setting the standards for the modern murder-mystery movie.

title: Glass Onion: A Knives Out Mystery

title: Glass Onion: A Knives Out Mystery
reviewText: This hilarious and intricately-plotted 'Knives Out' sequel is just as fun as the original —even if some of the characters and social satire don't pack quite the same punch.

title: Glass Onion: A Knives Out Mystery

title: Glass Onion: A Knives Out Mystery
reviewText: Reteaming Johnson with actor Daniel Craig as genteel master detective Benoit Blanc, it is a smart storytelling treat — and also the rare sequel that fully lives up to its promise.

title: Glass Onion: A Knives Out Mystery

title: Glass Onion: A Knives Out Mystery
reviewText: It is Janelle Monae who hits it far out like Ana de Armas in Knives Out when Johnson suddenly pulls the rabbit out of the hat in his super fun twisting and turning that tops the first film by far.

title: Glass Onion: A Knives Out Mystery

title: Glass Onion: A Knives Out Mystery
reviewText: The lack of regulations, the extreme wealth, and the few scruples of this particular class of nouveau riche is the background of this satire, which, although superficial, has a massive reach and leaves us questioning the facts.

title: Glass Onion: A Knives Out Mystery
reviewText: Craig and Monae have the most fun and give easily the best performances.

title: Glass Onion: A Knives Out Mystery
reviewText: The rest of the movie, though, tries so hard to top the sizzle of the original.

title: Glass Onion: A Knives Out Mystery
reviewText: This is a rich, perfectly orchestrated performance that could have gone wrong even with the slightest of misjudgement in character.

title: Glass Onion: A Knives Out Mystery
reviewText: That her performance gets even better in the second watch...

title: Glass Onion: A Knives Out Mystery
reviewText: All the money in the world, and "Glass Onion" never feels meaningfully engaged in any aesthetically definitive approach to a mystery instead it is overwhelmed by a strangely incurious approach to the people, the locales and itself.

title: Glass Onion: A Knives Out Mystery

title: Glass Onion: A Knives Out Mystery
reviewText: It seems to me an enjoyable, solvent sequel, whose surprise factor is as sharp as a piece of glass.

title: Glass Onion: A Knives Out Mystery

title: Glass Onion: A Knives Out Mystery
reviewText: Like Knives Out, Glass Onion fronts as a frothy potboiler just out for a good time.

title: Glass Onion: A Knives Out Mystery
reviewText: But in its heart, it's a scathing satire of our oligarchic ruling class.

title: Glass Onion: A Knives Out Mystery
reviewText: Benoit Blanc's return to the big screen brings with it another superb Rian Johnson whodunit, that seriously rivals the original Knives Out mystery.

title: Glass Onion: A Knives Out Mystery

title: Glass Onion: A Knives Out Mystery
reviewText: For all of the great satire of (GLASS ONION), the true brilliance of Rian Johnson's scrip, is that it creates this facade that suggests something deeper as it relates to Billionaire power and how they continue to create influence.

title: Glass Onion: A Knives Out Mystery
reviewText: Case solved and justice dispensed in bizarre, spectacular, and unconvincing ways.

title: Glass Onion: A Knives Out Mystery
reviewText: It is an entertaining and amusing movie, but a step down from its immediate predecessor.

title: Glass Onion: A Knives Out Mystery
reviewText: It is an entertaining and amusing movie, but a step down from its immediate predecessor.

title: Glass Onion: A Knives Out Mystery
reviewText: Linearly plotted, Glass Onion is stunningly unfunny and desperately wacky; a barely coherent mess moved by a silly game with no thrills and no real mystery.

title: Glass Onion: A Knives Out Mystery
reviewText: Suffers from sequel-itus: you hope for something better but you get something a little less.
